context: Tests whether the agent identifies Datadog as a custom provider requiring documentation lookup, avoids hardcoding credentials in provider blocks, sets correct provider version constraints, and documents the plan-then-apply CI/CD workflow.

checklist.name: Datadog provider version constraint
checklist.description: The Datadog provider uses a version constraint (e.g. ~> 3.0) in the required_providers block

checklist.name: No hardcoded AWS credentials
checklist.description: The AWS provider block does NOT contain access_key or secret_key attributes with literal string values

checklist.name: No hardcoded Datadog credentials
checklist.description: The Datadog provider block does NOT contain api_key or app_key as literal string values — they use variables or environment variable references

checklist.name: AWS region via variable
checklist.description: The AWS provider region attribute uses var.aws_region or a variable reference, NOT a hardcoded string

checklist.name: plan-out workflow documented
checklist.description: The output includes documentation that shows 'terraform plan -out=tfplan' followed by 'terraform apply tfplan' as two separate steps

checklist.name: Terraform version constraint present
checklist.description: The terraform block includes required_version with both a lower and upper bound

checklist.name: File organization correct
checklist.description: Generated files include at minimum: main.tf, variables.tf, outputs.tf, versions.tf

checklist.name: Sensitive variables marked
checklist.description: Variables that hold credentials (api_key, app_key) have sensitive = true

checklist.name: Variable descriptions present
checklist.description: All variable declarations include a description attribute
